citation.sourceDocument: Hawaii DoT 2026 rate schedule (HRS §235-51, 12 brackets 1.4–11%); Act 46 SLH 2024 standard deduction $8,000/$16,000/$12,000; $1,144 exemption
citation.sourceNote: Hawaii has the most income-tax brackets of any state — TWELVE, from 1.40% to 11.00% (HRS §235-51), making it the deepest graduated schedule the engine models (beating Delaware's seven). The 12 rates — 1.40% / 3.20% / 5.50% / 6.40% / 6.80% / 7.20% / 7.60% / 7.90% / 8.25% / 9.00% / 10.00% / 11.00% — are uniform across filing statuses; only the thresholds differ, and they do so by a fixed statutory ratio: the married-filing-jointly schedule (Schedule II) is exactly 2× the single/MFS schedule (Schedule I) and the head-of-household schedule (Schedule III) is exactly 1.5× single (verified against both the 2024 published schedule and the Tax Foundation 2026 table — e.g. single's 3.20% tier begins at $9,600, joint's at $19,200, HoH's at $14,400). Act 46 (SLH 2024) — the largest income-tax cut in Hawaii history, phasing in through 2031 — widened the brackets for tax year 2025 (carried to 2026, the next widening is 2027) and raised the standard deduction again for 2026 to $8,000 (single / married filing separately), $16,000 (married jointly / surviving spouse), and $12,000 (head of household), up from the 2024-25 $4,400/$8,800/$6,424. On top of the deduction, Hawaii subtracts a $1,144-per-exemption personal exemption (single $1,144, joint $2,288, head of household $1,144), unchanged by Act 46. Married filing separately maps to single exactly (Schedule I, the $8,000 deduction, $1,144 exemption — not an approximation here). The engine needed no new capability — this is the existing brackets + standard deduction + personal exemption stack, just the deepest schedule. Worked example, single $60,000 AGI: taxable = 60,000 − 8,000 − 1,144 = $50,856; tax climbs the first seven bands to $2,756.26 (the marginal dollar sits in the 7.60% tier). The $1,144-per-dependent exemption, the age-65 doubled exemption, the refundable food/excise and earned-income credits, and the lower long-term capital-gains rate (7.25%, which the wage-based engine does not reach) are omitted at launch fidelity, so the figure errs slightly high — the conservative side. Hawaii levies no county/local income tax. The brackets and standard deduction step on Act 46's statutory schedule; the refresh adapter anchors the standard-deduction amounts and the per-status bracket tables roll alongside as the reviewer's data-only step. Verified against the Hawaii Department of Taxation tax-year information, HRS §235-51, Act 46 (SLH 2024), and the Tax Foundation 2026 state-rate table on 2026-06-06.
